title: BMS Safety Architecture Review — 2023-07-01
text:
# BMS Safety Architecture Review — 2023-07-01

**Space:** K200  
**Meeting type:** Safety Review  
**Attendees:** Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE), Lena Okafor (Power), Rowan Ellery (Electrical), Tyler Moss (Firmware), Aisha Mbeki (Test), Nina Schulz (Quality)  
**Chair:** Dr. Rhea Voss  
**Minutes recorded by:** Nina Schulz

---

## Purpose

This review was convened to establish the safety architecture for the BMS module (P-1075) ahead of the DOC-431 BMS Safety Specification release. The March 2023 bench test series identified two safety-relevant observations that needed to be captured in the specification before the BMS hardware was finalised.

## March 2023 Bench Test Findings

Lena Okafor presented the relevant test data:

1. **SOC floor (18% minimum):** Below 18% SOC under a 3C discharge rate, one of the six series cells showed a measured cell voltage reversal of 12mV lasting approximately 40 seconds. Cell reversal is a precursor to separator failure and thermal event. The thermal model (run by Lena Okafor, data in internal test log reference BT-2023-03-12) shows that a 6-point margin (18% floor vs the 12% reversal-onset) provides the required safety buffer at all operating temperatures from -5°C to +45°C.

2. **SOC ceiling (82% maximum):** At SOC above 85% and charge rate above 0.5C with ambient below 5°C, localised lithium plating was observed on the anode surface of the graphite cells in the test batch (post-mortem optical microscopy). Plating is a fire risk. The 82% ceiling was selected to provide a 3-point margin below the plating onset at 85%.

## Architecture Decisions

**AGREED:** Implement the 18% SOC floor and 82% SOC ceiling as hardware limits in P-1075, not firmware-only. The BMS hardware lockout circuit must be independent of the main microcontroller to prevent single-point software fault bypass.

- Decision maker: Dr. Rhea Voss (CSE)
- ECR reference: ECR-209 (BMS parameter update, captures the specific threshold values from this meeting)
- Documentation: DOC-431 §4.3 must record the full rationale (Lena Okafor to draft, Dr. Voss to review)
- Review record: REV-623 (Dr. Voss review of DOC-431 v1.3) contains additional comments on this rationale

## Actions

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Update P-1075 BMS hardware design for independent lockout circuit | Rowan Ellery | 2023-08-01 |
| A2 | Draft DOC-431 §4.3 with full SOC limit rationale | Lena Okafor | 2023-07-21 |
| A3 | Raise ECR-209 to formally capture BMS parameter changes | Lena Okafor | 2023-07-10 |
| A4 | Review DOC-431 v1.3 | Dr. Rhea Voss | 2023-07-12 |
